shot.subject: tree
shot.notes: from the roots, the camera tilts up a colossal trunk into the crown

segments.text: In a world where grass stands over a meter tall, it takes ambition to be called a giant. These qualify.

shot.subject: tree
shot.notes: the full treeline against the sky, crowns towering over the grass ocean

segments.text: Above the swaying green nation, above every flier we have followed, the trees hold the only permanent skyline this garden has.

shot.subject: tree
shot.notes: a slow circle around one great crown, branches turning past the lens

segments.text: The trees.

shot.subject: tree
shot.notes: macro on bark, then travel up the trunk as it divides and divides

segments.text: A trunk, on its way up, makes its choices. Here it splits; each branch splits again; each of those again — the same simple rule, repeated toward the light, until a single stem has become a crown of hundreds.

shot.subject: tree
shot.notes: rise through the branching interior, twigs and leaves thickening around the lens

segments.text: No two trees in the garden share a shape, because a tree is not a design. It is a history — every branch a decision recorded in wood, every asymmetry a memory of wind or shade or chance. The Gardener plants the seed and sets its character; the growing does the rest.

shot.subject: tree
shot.notes: several trees side by side, each silhouette distinct

segments.text: That is why a treeline never repeats itself. It is not a row of objects. It is a row of biographies.

shot.subject: ladybug
shot.notes: a ladybug climbs past camera up the bark's canyons

segments.text: And every biography carries tenants. The bark is a vertical country — the ladybugs' uptown, a cliff face of canyons and ridges walked by anything with the legs for it.

shot.subject: web
shot.notes: glide between two trunks; a spider web spans the gap, sunlit

segments.text: The spaces between the trunks are prime real estate of a darker sort. The spiders rig the corridors, taxing the air traffic. An old tree in this garden anchors half the hunting economy of its neighborhood.

shot.subject: birds
shot.notes: the flock settles briefly above the canopy, then pours off it

segments.text: Above it all, the crowns give the birds their one solid landmark in a world of waving green. The grass is the garden's floor; the trees are its furniture, its towers, and its tenements — all at once.

shot.subject: tree
shot.notes: rising through the crown's interior: layer upon layer of leaves, light thinning

segments.text: A crown is a business built on one commodity. Light enters from the top; each layer of leaves takes its share; and what reaches the bottom branches is the dregs — a green twilight that barely pays for the leaves that catch it. A tree's shape is not a pose. It is a market, resolved.

shot.subject: tree
shot.notes: a leaf turning slowly on its stalk, tilting toward the brighter sky

segments.text: Every leaf negotiates its own position — angling on its stalk, edging toward gaps, spending the tree's substance to reach the tree's income. Branches that win the negotiation thicken. Branches that lose it are quietly abandoned: starved, dried, and eventually surrendered to the wind. The biography written in the crown's asymmetry includes its bankruptcies.

shot.subject: tree
shot.notes: two neighboring crowns, their edges shying just short of touching

segments.text: And where two giants stand close, watch the border between their crowns. The branches approach and hesitate, leaving a channel of daylight — each tree reading the other's shade, declining a war neither can win. The treeline's canopy is not one roof but a treaty of roofs, negotiated leaf by leaf over years.

shot.subject: tree
shot.notes: from the roots looking up: the trunk as a single column carrying the whole enterprise

segments.text: All of it — the market, the treaties, the tenants — rides on the column. The trunk carries the crown's entire payroll skyward and the canopy's entire income down, every day, through a structure that must also simply stand. It is worth remembering, whenever a tree seems idle: stillness, at this size, is a full-time job.

shot.subject: tree
shot.notes: a quiet frame; a single tree stands alone; something is about to change

segments.text: But this garden's giants live with a hazard no wild forest knows. The hand that plants them also, sometimes, brings them down.

shot.subject: stump
shot.notes: macro on a trunk's base as a clean cut opens across it

segments.text: When the Gardener chooses to fell a tree, the cut is unlike anything nature makes — a single plane, drawn straight through the living wood. The trunk above it lets go of the trunk below.

shot.subject: tree
shot.notes: the crown tips, gathers speed, and comes down through frame; grass erupts

segments.text: And then, gravity's paragraph. A lifetime of patient branching spends itself in seconds. The crown that hosted ladybugs and anchored webs sweeps down through the air lanes and strikes the meadow with a violence this quiet world rarely sees.

shot.subject: stump
shot.notes: from above: the flat, ringed stump and the fallen crown beside it

segments.text: What remains tells the whole story: a stump inscribed with its rings — the tree's own diary, cut open at the last page — and a fallen giant already becoming something else: a bridge for ants, a windbreak for seedlings, cover for anything small.

shot.subject: stump
shot.notes: glide low across the fallen trunk, tenants beginning to explore it

segments.text: Nothing here mourns, and nothing wastes. Within moments of the fall, the vertical country becomes a horizontal one, and the tenants file their change of address. In the garden, even catastrophe is real estate.

shot.subject: field
shot.notes: the gap in the treeline; light pours through onto suddenly bright grass

segments.text: And the gap itself is a gift. Light that the crown once hoarded now floods the floor. Grass thickens; flowers rush the clearing. A felled tree spends its final inheritance as sunshine.

shot.subject: tree
shot.notes: a young tree at the clearing's edge, thin and upright, growing

segments.text: At the clearing's margin, the next biography has already started — a sapling with its whole architecture ahead of it, writing its first branch. The skyline heals the way skylines do: one story at a time.

shot.subject: field
shot.notes: sweep from the treeline down across the meadow to a scatter of gray shapes

segments.text: The trees are the garden's tallest lives, and among its longest. But for true patience — for residents who watched every tree in this garden arrive, and will watch every one of them fall — we must visit the only citizens that were never alive at all.

shot.subject: rock
shot.notes: a lichen-gray boulder, ancient and immovable, grass leaning against it

segments.text: Next: the rocks.
